behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff between evaluate-stage writer and artifact store (`MOCK-MISS` yes). No real OS/network/subprocess required (`REAL-INFRA` no). No correctness dependence on model judgment; this is artifact key routing (`LLM-DEP` no). Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about where data is stored, not quality of model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with deterministic input and an in-process artifact repository; assert one new write to `grounding`, zero writes to `plan`, and preserved payload/metadata.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Needs wiring across implement flow + artifact reader/writer (`MOCK-MISS` yes). No OS/network/subprocess dependency (`REAL-INFRA` no). Can validate read-path and write target with deterministic fixtures, so model-output quality is not required (`LLM-DEP` no for this behavior). Rejected Agentic because behavior text specifies read/write orchestration, not evaluating generated content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run-context artifacts with `grounding`; execute implement flow using deterministic model stub; assert grounding was read (e.g., injected into input context) and final artifact write goes to `plan` only.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at interaction of deep-dive control flow and artifact persistence, not a single pure function (`MOCK-MISS` yes). No real infra needed (`REAL-INFRA` no). Condition is based on provided issue content presence, not model quality (`LLM-DEP` no). Rejected Agentic because behavior text disqualifies output-quality checks and only targets side-effect suppression.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with in-process artifact store, assert no `plan` overwrite occurs and existing `plan` artifact remains unchanged.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: This is state handoff across local modules (lineage tracking + implement resolver), so unit is too low (`MOCK-MISS` yes). No requirement for real OS/network/subprocess (`REAL-INFRA` no). No requirement to validate model judgment (`LLM-DEP` no). Rejected Workflow because behavior text asks lineage preservation/readability, not end-to-end multi-agent outcome quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate-stage write to `grounding` in a run context, then invoke implement-stage resolver in same context; assert lineage metadata links and implement retrieves the exact prior artifact version.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multiple sequential agentic stages and cross-stage grounding influence (`MULTI-STEP` yes). A stubbed deterministic chain could hide failures where model decisions fail to carry grounding (`MOCK-HIDE` yes). Rejected lower levels because text requires full run outcome across `deep-dive -> ... -> implement`, not isolated module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real multi-step pipeline end-to-end in one run context; assert final `plan` content includes/uses salient facts from earlier `grounding` artifact and that artifact trail shows expected stage sequence.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Requires implement fallback logic plus artifact store state integrity checks across modules (`MOCK-MISS` yes). No real infra needed (`REAL-INFRA` no). Can be validated with deterministic fixtures; no model-output quality dependence required (`LLM-DEP` no). Rejected Agentic because behavior text is safety/fallback and artifact integrity, not judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Start with run context lacking `grounding` but containing other artifacts; run implement flow; assert a valid new `plan` artifact is produced, no unrelated artifacts are modified/deleted, and schema/metadata remain valid.
